content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a game with an array AAA of NNN integers. The rules of the game are as follows:


Let ‚ą£A‚ą£|A|‚ą£A‚ą£ denote the current length of AAA.
If ‚ą£A‚ą£‚Č§2|A| \le 2‚ą£A‚ą£‚Č§2, the game terminates immediately.

Otherwise, Alice must choose an index iii such that 1<i<‚ą£A‚ą£1 < i < |A|1<i<‚ą£A‚ą£.
Then, Bob must choose **one** of the following two actions:

Delete all elements at indices 1,2,‚Ä¶,i1, 2, \dots, i1,2,‚Ä¶,i.
Delete all elements at indices i,i+1,‚Ä¶,‚ą£A‚ą£i, i+1, \dots, |A|i,i+1,‚Ä¶,‚ą£A‚ą£.


After Bob's choice, the remaining elements are re-indexed starting from 111.
After the game terminates, Alice's score is defined to be the largest element remaining in the array.

Note that the game is only allowed to end when there are no more than two elements remaining in AAA.

Alice wants to maximize her score, while Bob wants to minimize Alice's score.

Assuming both players play optimally, let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote Alice's final score for the array AAA.

You must report the sum of answers across all subarrays of AAA, i.e. the value

‚ąĎL=1N‚ąĎR=LNf(A[L,R])\sum_{L=1}^N \sum_{R=L}^N f(A[L, R])L=1‚ąĎN‚ÄčR=L‚ąĎN‚Äčf(A[L,R])where A[L,R]=[AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,AR]A[L, R] = [A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_R]A[L,R]=[AL‚Äč,AL+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äč] denotes the subarray of AAA from index LLL to RRR.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line contains a single integer NNN, denoting the initial size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer representing Alice's score under optimal play, summed up across all subarrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
